title: cordiera elliptica
intro: Cordiera elliptica, also known as Yerba Santa, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant's leaves have been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds. Cordiera elliptica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal benefits, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
